message:
test(ft-xbnl0.2.4): POST does not auto-inject Content-Type (tick 398)

Added `distributed_http_client_post_does_not_auto_inject_content_type`
— pins that `DistributedHttpClient::post` treats the body as opaque
bytes and does NOT auto-inject a `Content-Type` header.

Why matters: distributed RPC endpoints rely on explicit request
shapes. An auto-injected `Content-Type: application/json` would
misroute bincode / msgpack / protobuf bodies at servers that
content-negotiate. The client should be transport-only, not
payload-inspecting.

Test sends a POST with a JSON body (valid JSON, but incidentally —
the client shouldn't peek). Server asserts the received request
lines (case-insensitive) do NOT contain "content-type:". If
asupersync's HTTP client starts setting a default Content-Type,
this test fires — the bead author can then decide whether to pin
the default explicitly or override it.

Complements tick 325 (Host header pinned explicitly) + tick 326
(empty-body POST Content-Length: 0). Now three request-header
shapes are pinned:
- Host: matches URL authority (tick 325)
- Content-Length: 0 for empty body (tick 326)
- Content-Type: NOT auto-set by the client (this tick)

Test result: 1 passed; 0 failed
Compile time: 1m 15s cold.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
